scanner_description: Detects patterns AI tools generate that ship to production — SQL concatenation, missing awaits on auth checks, weak fallbacks, commented-out security, debug modes, default Docker creds.

rules.name: jwt.decode() Used Instead of jwt.verify()
rules.note: jwt.decode() does not validate the signature. Anyone can forge a valid-looking token. AI tools confuse decode() and verify() constantly.
rules.remediation: Replace jwt.decode(token) with jwt.verify(token, secret, { algorithms: ['HS256'] })

rules.name: SQL Query Built from String Concatenation
rules.note: User input concatenated into SQL query — the #1 AI-generated vulnerability. Looks functional, fully exploitable via SQL injection.
rules.remediation: Use parameterized queries: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1', [userId])

rules.name: Env Variable with Weak Fallback Default
rules.note: process.env.X || 'secret' — if env var missing in prod, app silently uses the weak default. AI adds this for dev convenience.
rules.remediation: Remove the fallback. Crash on startup if required env vars are missing: if (!process.env.JWT_SECRET) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET required')

rules.name: bcrypt Rounds Set Too Low
rules.note: bcrypt cost factor under 10 — brute-forceable in seconds on modern hardware. AI sets low rounds for 'speed during development.'
rules.remediation: Use minimum 12 rounds: bcrypt.genSalt(12). Recommended: 12-14 for 2024+ hardware.

rules.name: Session Secret Set to Placeholder Value
rules.note: Session secret is a well-known placeholder. AI generates these — they ship to production and allow session forging.

rules.name: TODO: Auth/Security Not Implemented
rules.note: Comment indicates security feature was planned but never implemented. Common AI pattern: generate a TODO, move on, ship.
rules.remediation: Implement the security feature before deploying. Don't ship TODOs.

rules.name: Auth Middleware Commented Out
rules.note: Authentication middleware is present but commented out. Likely disabled during testing and never re-enabled.
rules.remediation: Uncomment the auth middleware. Use environment-based bypasses instead of commenting out.

rules.name: console.log of Sensitive Data
rules.note: Debug logging of passwords, tokens, or secrets left in code. Extremely common AI-generated debug pattern.
rules.remediation: Remove console.log of sensitive data. Use structured logging with redaction in production.

rules.name: Async Auth Check Without Await
rules.note: Auth check called but not awaited — returns Promise<true> which is truthy, so auth always passes. Silent bypass.
rules.remediation: Add await: const isAuth = await checkAuth(req). Ensure the check blocks the handler.

rules.name: CORS Set to Reflect Origin or Wildcard
rules.note: CORS reflects the requesting origin or uses wildcard '*'. Reflection nullifies CORS protection (critical when paired with credentials). Wildcard is sometimes intentional on public read-only endpoints.
rules.remediation: Set explicit allowed origins: cors({ origin: ['https://yourdomain.com'] }). If wildcard is intentional on a public read-only endpoint (badges, public stats), suppress as intentional.

rules.name: Missing await on Security Check in Condition
rules.note: Async security check used in if() without await. Promise object is truthy — auth always passes regardless of actual result.

rules.name: Debug Mode in Production Config (NODE_ENV=development)
rules.note: NODE_ENV set to development in a config file that may be deployed to production. Exposes stack traces, internal paths, and debug endpoints.

rules.name: Debug Mode in Production Config (FLASK_DEBUG=1)
rules.note: Flask debug enabled in a config file that may be deployed to production. Exposes the Werkzeug debugger, which allows arbitrary code execution.

rules.name: Debug Mode in Production Config (DEBUG=True)
rules.note: Python DEBUG=True in a config file that may be deployed to production. Common Django/Flask pitfall.

rules.name: Debug Mode in Production Config (APP_DEBUG=true)
rules.note: Laravel APP_DEBUG=true in a config file that may be deployed to production. Exposes stack traces, env vars, and DB queries.

rules.name: Default Database Credentials in Docker Config
rules.note: Default/weak database password in docker-compose.yml or Dockerfile. If this file is committed, anyone can access the database.
rules.remediation: Use a strong random password. Reference env vars: ${DB_PASSWORD} with .env file.

rules.name: Firebase Open Read/Write Rules
rules.note: Firebase rules allow read/write to anyone. Every person on the internet can read and write your entire database.
rules.remediation: Replace with ownership-based rules: allow read, write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;
